prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene were computed using DFT at tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, exact four-center integrals, PySCF), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The geometry was the user-supplied .xyz file (no relaxation); Fukui indices were derived from Mulliken partial charges of the neutral singlet, cation (N−1, doublet), and anion (N+1, doublet) states, all SCF-converged. The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (C at the ipso position, directly bonded to the NO₂ group) with f⁺ = 0.496; the most nucleophilic site is atom 4 (C at the ortho position) with f⁻ = 0.107. Per-atom Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptors are provided in the full output. Charge conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = Σf⁻ = 1.0). No warnings reported.</prose>
